notes: Ravi Shankar (sitarist): Awards - Bharat Ratna (1999), Padma Vibhushan (1981), Padma Bhushan (1967). His Other Books: ‘My Music, My Life’; ‘Yours in Music’. Bharatanatyam. Kamala Lakshmi Narayanan was invited to perform for Queen Elizabeth II during her coronation festivities in 1953. Awards: Kalaimamani (1967), Padma Bhushan (1970). She is a preeminent artist of the Vazhuvoor tradition of the dance. In 2010 she received a National Heritage Fellowship from the National Endowment for the Arts for her contributions to the arts.

Purandara Dasa composed thousands of devotional songs, known as 'Devaranamas', in praise of Lord Vishnu. The original name of Purandara Das was Srinivasa Nayaka. Vyasatirtharu was the guru of Purandara Dasa.

Sadir. R Muthukannammal is a seventh generation veteran Sadir dancer from the Indian State of Tamil Nadu. She is the only surviving person among the 32 Devadasis who served the deity at the Viralimalai Murugan temple. She received the Padma Bhushan in 1969, the Padma Vibhushan in 1999, the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1989, and the 'Officier de la Legion d'Honneur,' France's highest civilian award, in 2009. Manna Dey was a renowned Indian playback singer, music director, and musician. He had a classical music background, being part of the Bhendibazaar Gharana. He received Padma Shri in 1971, Padma Bhushan in 2005 and Dadasaheb Phalke award in 2007. Q.155. Ustad Zakir Hussain (Tabla player). His Awards : Padma Vibhushan (2023); Padma bhusan (2002); Padma Shri (1988); Sangeet Natak Akademi (1991). Q.157. Kirana. Gangu Bai Hangal was an Indian singer of the khayal genre of Hindustani classical music from Karnataka. She received the Padma Shri in 1962, the Padma Bhushan in 1971, and the Padma Vibhushan in 2002.

Yakshagana dance. It is a traditional folk dance form popular in Coastal Karnataka districts. Chittani Ramachandra Hegde was awarded the Padma Shri award in 2012. Some exponents of Yakshagana : Kuriya Vithala Shastry, Soorikumeru Govinda Bhat, Narayana Hasyagar.

Padma Vibhushan. Pandit Hariprasad Chaurasia belonged to the Senia gharana. Awards: Sangeet Natak Academy (1983), Konark Samman (1992), Padma Bhushan (1992). Other Flute Players: Devendra Murdeshwar, Raghunath Seth, Nityanand Haldipur, Ronu Majumdar, Pt. Rajendra Prasanna, and Chetan Joshi.

Tamil Nadu. Vani Jairam was an Indian playback singer, fondly referred to as the "Meera of Modern India." She received the Padma Bhushan award in 2023. M.S. Subbulakshmi Award is a tribute to the legendary Carnatic singer, M.S. Subbulakshmi. She was the first musician ever to be awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1998.

Odissi. Ranjana Gauhar also received the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 2007. Other renowned Odissi dancers include Kelucharan Mohapatra, Mayadhar Raut, Sanjukta Panigrahi, Ratikant Mohapatra, Deba Prasad Das, Kumkum Mohanty, Pankaj Charan Das, and Aruna Mohanty.

Chintaman Raghunath Vyas was an Indian classical singer, renowned for his khyal style. He was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 1992 and the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 1987. He researched traditional ragas and bandishes, composing over bandishes in various ragas under the pen name Gunijaan. In tribute to his guru, Gunidas, he founded the Gunidas Sangeet Sammelan in 1977.

Lavani. Sulochana Chavan : Her Awards - Padma Shri (in 2022), Lata Mangeshkar Award (in 2010), Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (in 2012). Other Lavani Singers : Bela Shende, Asha Bhosle, Usha Mangeshkar, Vaishali Samant. Q.165. Identify the Indian classical dancer associated with Kathak. Awards: Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1979), Maharashtra Gaurav Puraskar (1990), Kalidas Samman (2001), Sangeet Natak Akademi Fellowship (2006).

Assam. Bhupen Hazarika (Sudhakantha) was a singer known for his baritone voice. He received the National Film Award for Best Music Direction in 1975, the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1987), Bharat Ratna (2019), Padma Vibhushan (2012), Padma Bhushan (2001), and Padma Shri (1977), Dada Saheb Phalke Award (1992), Asom Ratna (2009).

Sattriya. Indira P. P. Bora has won Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 1996. Dance and their exponents : Sattriya - Sharodi Saikia, Guru Jatin Goswami, Guru Ghanakanta Bora, Manik Barbayan, Pushpa Bhuyan.

Ustad Amjad Ali Khan Bangash: Awards - Padma Shri (1975), Padma Bhushan (1991), Padma Vibhushan (2001). Uttar Pradesh. Girija Devi was a Thumri singer of the Senia and Banaras gharanas. She was dubbed as the 'Queen of Thumri' for her contribution in the genre. Ravi Shankar. He was an Indian musician and player of the sitar, who was influential in stimulating Western appreciation of Indian music. His Awards: Bharat Ratna (1999), Padma Vibhushan (1981), Padma Bhushan (1967) and Grammy Award (1968) for Best Chamber Music Performance for his work "West Meets East".

Maihar gharana. Ali Akbar Khan (Sarod) : Awards - Padma Bhushan (1967), Padma Vibhushan (1989). Exponents of Maihar gharana : Allauddin Khan, Pandit Nikhil Ranjan Banerjee, Nityanand Haldipur. Lagaan. Asha Bhosle: She was officially acknowledged as the most recorded artist in music history by the Guinness Book of World Records in 2011. Her Award: Dadasaheb Phalke Award (2000), Padma Vibhushan (2008), Banga Bibhushan (2018), Maharashtra Bhushan (2021), Filmfare Lifetime Achievement Award (2001).
Bade Ghulam Ali Khan. He was an Indian vocalist from the Kasur Patiala Gharana. His Award: Padma Bhushan (1962), and Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1962).

Carnatic music. Paapanaasam Raamayya Sivan was awarded the Sangeetha Kalanidhi by the Madras Music Academy in 1971. He also composed film scores for Kannada and Tamil cinema in the 1930s and 1940s and was known as Tamil Thyagaraja. Amir Khusrow (1253-1325) is credited with creating qawwali, while Tukadoji Maharaj introduced the Bhajan and the Kanjiri Bhajan, performed with the Kanjiri instrument.

Sangeet Natak Akademi Award. Also known as the Akademi Puraskar, this award honors excellence in the performing arts. The santoor, a trapezoid-shaped hammered dulcimer made of walnut, is popular in India.

Padma Vibhushan. Ustad Amjad Ali Khan (Sarod Samrat): He is an Indian classical sarod player, best known for his clear and fast ekhara taans. He was born in Gwalior. Awards: Padma Shri (1975), Padma Bhushan (1991), Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1989), Sangeet Natak Akademi Fellowship (2011).

M Balamuralikrishna was a renowned Indian vocalist, composer, and innovator in Carnatic music. This award is presented every year to those who have devoted their life in understanding and promoting social harmony. It constitutes a citation and cash Prize of Rs. 10 Lakh.

Lucknow Gharana was established under the patronage of Wajid Ali Shah, the last Nawab of Oudh. This gharana is characterized by its emphasis on grace and elegance. Other Kathak Gharana and Founder: Jaipur Gharana - Bhanuji; Benaras Gharana - Janaki Prasad; Raigarh Gharana - Raja Chakradhar Singh.
Shivkumar Sharma was an Indian classical musician and santoor player. His Other Awards: Padma Vibhushan (2001), Padma Shri (1991).

Baiga Dance Form: It is a traditional art form of the Baiga tribe, an indigenous community in Madhya Pradesh. Uday Shankar. He is known as the Father of Modern Dance in India. Awards - Padma Vibhushan (1971), Sangeet Natak Akademi Fellowship (1962).

Kirana Musical Family. It was founded by Abdul Karim Khan. Khayal gayaki and Dhrupad-Dhamar are combined to create the gayaki of the Agra Gharana. Chhannulal Mishra was awarded the Padma Bhushan in 2010, Padma Vibhushan in 2020.
Odissi. Awards of Sanjukta Panigrahi: Padma Shri (1975) and Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1976). Odissi dance exponents: Kelucharan Mohapatra, Kumkum Mohanty, Protima Bedi, Sujata Mohapatra.

Bharatnatyam (Tamil Nadu). Awards of Chitra Visweswaran: Padma Shri (1992). Notable Bharatanatyam dancers include Rukmini Devi Arundale, Padma Subrahmanyam, Mallika Sarabhai, Yamini Krishnamurthy, Mrinalini Sarabhai, Alarmel Valli, Bala Saraswati, Anita Ratnam, Sonal Mansingh, and Sudha Chandran.

Pandit Vishnu Narayan Bhatkhande. He was a prominent musician who wrote the first modern treatise on Hindustani classical music. In 1916, he reorganized the Baroda state music school. He set up Marris College of Music in Lucknow in 1926, which is now known as Bhatkhande Music Institute. He published books Swar Malika and Shri Mallakshaya Sangeetam (pen name Chatur Pandit).

Agra Gharana. Vijay Kichlu was an Indian classical singer. Award - Padma Shri (2018). Agra Gharana was founded by Haji Sujan Khan and Ustad Gagghe Khuda Bakhsh. Other Exponents of Agra Gharana - Faiyaz Khan, Latafat Hussain Khan, Dinkar Kaikini and Lalith Rao.

Manipuri dance. Thiyam Suryamukhi Devi - She created her own solo composition such as Basanta Barnar Sakhi Ukti. Other Manipuri Exponents : Guru Bipin Singh, Maisnam Amubi Singh, Rajkumar Singhajit Singh, Sougaijam Thanil Singh, Thingbaijam Babu Singh, Darshana Jhaveri, etc.

Birju Maharaj. He is the direct descendant of Ishwari Prasad (the first known Kathak teacher). Birju Maharaj established an institution named ‘Kalashram’ for imparting training in dance and associated disciplines. Awards - Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1964), Padma Vibhushan (1986).
Darshana Jhaveri, the youngest of the four Jhaveri sisters, is a disciple of Guru Bipin Singh. Awards - Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1996), Padma Shri (2002), Kalidas Samman Award (2018).
Chathunni Panicker: Awards - Padma Shri (2006), Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1973). Other exponents of Kathakali - Kanak Rele, Kottakkal Sivaraman, Guru Kunchu Kurup.

Darbhanga. Siyaram Tiwari was an esteemed Indian classical singer and a prominent exponent of the Dhrupad genre in Hindustani classical music. Award : Padma Shri (1971). Notable singers from this gharana include Ram Chatur Mallick, Pandit Vidur Mallick, Padmashri Pandit Ram Kumar Mallick, and Pt. Prem Kumar Mallik.

Anita Ratnam, a classically trained Bharatanatyam dancer, has also received formal training in Kathakali, Mohiniattam, tai chi, and Kalarippayattu. She combined these diverse influences to create her unique dance style, which she calls "Neo Bharatam."

Lucknow. Acchan Maharaj is the father of Pandit Birju Maharaj. Exponents of Lucknow Gharana - Pandit Birju Maharaj, Shambhu Maharaj, Lachhu Maharaj (Pandit Bajinath Prasad), Pandit Ishwari Prasad, Kalika Prasad, Manjari Chaturvedi.

Kathak. Sitara Devi (Dhanalakshmi): She was an Indian dancer of the classical Kathak style of dancing, a singer, and an actress. She was born at Cuttack, Odisha. Rabindranath Tagore described her as Nritya Samragni meaning the empress of dance. Awards - Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (1969), the Padma Shri (1973).

Abdul Karim Khan : He was an Indian classical singer. He introduced Hindustani Khayal singing and Indian classical music in Kirana gharana. Bismillah Khan. He was a Hindustani classical shehnai player. His awards: Bharat Ratna (2001), Padma Vibhushan (1980), Padma Bhushan (1968), Padma Shri (1961). Sangeet Natak Akademi has decided to institute “Ustad Bismillah Khan Yuva Puraskar” from the year 2006. Awards of Lata Mangeskar: Padma Bhushan (1969), Padma Vibhushan (1999), Bharat Ratna (2001).

He was a renowned Marathi singer and stage actor, famous for portraying female characters in Marathi plays at a time when women were not permitted to act on stage. Natya Sangeet is a form of drama that combines prose and poetry through songs to convey the story, akin to musicals.
